fields.description:
## Why

R1 (asserted-capability probe), R3 (decomposition-shape), and R4 (necessity gate) all gate on
DETECTION POWER, which can only be measured against plans whose defects are KNOWN. This story
builds that ground truth by CONSTRUCTION: take real cleanly-closed plans, seed exactly one defect
per class, and the mutation itself is the label — no fresh LLM adjudication (which would duplicate
the gate's own sidecar and be circular; that is why E1's finding-level LLM labeling was
invalidated). The unmutated originals are the controls that bound blocking false positives under
the Tricorder ~10%-blocking-FP trust cliff.

This is the falsifiable experiment named verbatim in `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md`
§6 R3 (lines 183): "Seeded-flaw eval: take 10 real historical epics that closed cleanly; generate
mutated variants — re-sliced into layer-cake children, and with one ordering edge deleted where
consumption exists. Pass condition: ≥7/10 mutants flagged, ≤1/10 originals flagged (i.e., ≥87%
discrimination). This reuses the review machinery's batch runner; mutation is mechanical." E2
generalizes that one class to all five defect classes the epic names.

**How E2 feeds the epic's result gates.** E2 produces the labeled-positive corpus + a CURRENT-gate
baseline table; it is the reference point the amended-gate R-rows are judged against, NOT itself a
gate change (no `src/rebar` edit). It is consumed jointly:
- **R1 (59b7)** result gate — asserted-capability detection (class 1) is judged on E2's class-1
  mutants TOGETHER WITH E3's counterfactual replay (the two halves of R1's evidence).
- **R3 (3a27)** result gate — layer-cake + consumed-artifact-ordering detection (classes 2, 3) is
  judged on E2's class-2/3 mutants TOGETHER WITH E6's judge-reliability (E6 certifies the flag
  labels E2's discrimination number is computed from).
- **R4 (03a9)** consumes E2's class-4 (unnecessary-mechanism) mutants.
- **R2 (b080)** — class-5 (untagged operational AC). The current gate CANNOT detect this class (see
  ## What: `p6_ac_quality` is an FP-suppressor for the tag, not a missing-tag detector), so E2's
  class-5 baseline is ~0 BY DESIGN — that recorded gap is exactly what motivates R2's new
  operator-attested evidence-kind DET lint, which DOES NOT EXIST YET. E2 commits the class-5 mutant
  corpus + labels + the ~0 current-gate baseline; the class-5 detection gate is measured when R2
  lands and re-runs this harness with its lint (mirroring E5↔R5).

**Coherence for ALL classes (E2 delivers the reference, not the candidate's pass).** E2's deliverable
for every class is: the mutant CORPUS + labels, the pre-registered THRESHOLDS, the HARNESS, and the
CURRENT-gate BASELINE (how today's packaged gate scores mutants/controls). For the MISS classes
(1 absent-capability, 4 unnecessary-mechanism, 5 untagged-operational-AC) the current-gate baseline
detection is EXPECTED to be low/near-zero — that is the point (the gap motivating R1/R4/R2). Each
R-candidate's detection number is produced at THAT R-story's implementation by re-running this
harness with the candidate detector (as R1/R3/R4/R2 each are). E2's OWN acceptance is therefore
about the corpus/baseline/harness/thresholds being committed and construct-valid — NOT about any
candidate hitting its threshold.

Prior art the method rests on is external + methodological, NOT the in-repo pilots: the existing
harnesses (`seedpilot.py`, `harness.py`, `run_gate.py`, `final_gate.py`, `exp1_substance.py`)
hand-roll `anthropic.messages.create` with plan text in the prompt and do NO code-grounding, and
`exp2_agentic.py` grounds ad-hoc text via a bespoke file-tool agent — NONE is the production gate,
so none is reused or cited as a gate-run precedent (E2 introduces a new, verified gate-run
mechanism — see ## What). The method itself rests on mutation-testing's seeded-defect discrimination
tradition and the §6 R3 recommendation verbatim, with the shape checks grounded in CodePlan's
integration-truth argument (layer-cake) and claude-task-master's lower-ID-only DAG / Kiro's waves
(consumption-ordering), cited in §6 R3.

## What

Build a seeded-defect benchmark from real cleanly-closed plans drawn from the E1 outcome corpus
(`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`, 628 rows, 9-key schema per
`runs/README.md:53-66`), run the CURRENT plan-review gate over it, and commit the corpus + labels +
pre-registered thresholds + baseline detection/FP table. Five defect classes, each mutated
mechanically from a real base plan:

1. **absent asserted capability** — plan asserts an existing named module provides a capability it
   lacks (the E1-verified MISS class: dc58-af7b / db7b-c8fd / 5886-d028).
2. **layer-cake split** — children re-partitioned by architectural layer (schema/backend/frontend/
   tests) so no single child yields an end-to-end observable outcome.
3. **consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge** — a child's text names an artifact/interface a
   sibling creates, with NO `depends_on`/`blocks` edge or explicit sequencing.
4. **unnecessary mechanism** — the mutation injects exactly ONE new checklist/subtask line that
   introduces a NAMED mechanism the plan does not need and that no other part of the plan references or
   requires: `assemble_e2_mutants.py` appends one item drawn from a fixed, rotation-indexed template set
   — `{a caching layer, a new database index, an extra schema migration, a new background/cron job, a
   new third-party library dependency}` (selected deterministically by `hash(base_id) % len(templates)`
   so the mutation is mechanical and reproducible, not a judgment call). The injected mechanism is
   self-contained (introduces a noun no sibling/AC consumes), so the mutant differs from its control by
   exactly this one over-action line. ("**FixedBench over-action**" names the FixedBench-benchmark
   failure mode in which an agent adds machinery beyond the task's actual requirements; the seeded line
   emulates precisely that pattern — scope the plan never called for.)
5. **untagged operational AC** — an AC whose completion evidence lives outside the codebase; the
   mutation STRIPS the `[operator-attested]` tag from an AC that carries it (so the base MUST already
   have such an AC — see the class-5 base requirement below), leaving an untagged operational AC.

**"Cleanly-closed" is a precise, codebase-grounded filter** (defect class had no def before). A base
ticket qualifies iff its `outcome_corpus.jsonl` row satisfies ALL of:
`post_claim_edit_class == "none" AND reopen_count == 0 AND force_close == False AND
completion_verifier_fail_count == 0 AND had_persisted_review == True`
(every field is a real corpus key — verified against `runs/README.md:57-66`), AND — because the
corpus records no live status or child count — its live status is `closed` (from `rebar show`) AND,
for the container-shaped classes 2 & 3, it has ≥2 children (from `rebar list --parent`). This filter
selects **515 clean tickets, of which 292 are containers (31 epic + 261 story)** — verified by
running the filter over the committed corpus — so classes 1–4 have ≥10 distinct base plans
comfortably. Bugs are EXCLUDED as base plans (plan-review-exempt: `review_plan` returns PASS
runner=exempt for `bug`/`session_log`).

**Class-5 base requirement (the tag must exist to be stripped).** A class-5 base must ALSO already
carry ≥1 `[operator-attested]` AC line in its description. Verified prevalence (via `rebar search
"operator-attested"`, replay-derived full-text, cross-referenced to the clean filter): 43 tickets
have an `[operator-attested]` AC line, of which **12 are cleanly-closed** (8 containers = 7 story + 1
epic, plus 4 tasks). Class 5 draws from these 12 (tasks are admissible — the class-5 mutation is a
plan-text edit needing no children). 12 distinct clean bases ≥ 10, but it is tight, so the class-5
degradation ladder is explicitly wired: if fewer than 10 distinct suitable bases survive inspection,
(a) strip a DIFFERENT operator-attested AC from a base that has several (still construct-valid — the
mutant differs from its control by exactly one strip), else (b) run class 5 at the available N≥5 and
record the shortfall in the README. Each class-5 control is the base WITH the tag intact.

**The CURRENT-gate baseline is the reference point, not a pass claim.** The detectors R1/R2/R3/R4
propose do not yet exist in the shipped gate. E2 records, per class, what today's gate does; for the
MISS classes the baseline is expected low/near-zero, and that recorded gap is the point. Detection is
attributed by CRITERION FAMILY (mutant vs. its control differ by exactly one seeded defect, so a
family firing only on the mutant is attributable to the defect), using the current gate's nearest
existing criteria, each validated against the live registry (`plan_review.registry.by_id`) at run
time so no non-existent id is asserted. Class 5 has NO current detector — the R2-candidate lint is
the detector, benchmarked against this corpus when R2 lands:

| Class | Current-gate nearest criteria (baseline) | Expected baseline | Candidate detector (benchmarked later) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 absent capability | `E4`, `G1G2`, `G6` (agentic) | low | R1 asserted-capability probe |
| 2 layer-cake | `G3`, `G4` (container) | partial | R3 vertical-slice check |
| 3 consumed-artifact / ordering | `G4` (container) | partial | R3 consumption-vs-ordering check |
| 4 unnecessary mechanism | `E4`, `A1`, `G6` (agentic) | low | R4 necessity gate |
| 5 untagged operational AC | **none** — `p6_ac_quality` (det_floor.py:686) does lexical AC-quality checks only and holds NO operator-attested logic; the tag logic in `workflow_ops.py:54-59` (ADR-0043) is a FP-SUPPRESSOR that CLEARS `ac_unverifiable` when the tag IS present, and `passes.py:770` only coaches — nothing flags a MISSING tag | **~0 by design** (the R2 gap) | R2 operator-attested evidence-kind DET lint (does not exist yet) |

**Pre-registered per-class thresholds (the TARGET each candidate detector must hit when it is
benchmarked against this corpus at its R-story — NOT E2's own pass condition).** There is NO single
epic "gate table" (the epic states thresholds live in child ACs), so E2 pins them itself in
`runs/e2_thresholds.json`, committed BEFORE any detector run. Defaults are the §6 R3 discrimination
bar (≥7/10 mutants flagged, ≤1/10 controls flagged); classes 1 and 4 are pinned STRICTER to match
their child ACs (R1 59b7: ≥80% detection / ≤10% blocking-FP; R4 03a9: ≥8/10 / ≤1/10). The current
gate is not expected to meet these (that is the gap); the numbers gate the R-candidates later:

| Class | detection target (mutants) | blocking-FP bound (controls) | judged by / matches |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ≥8/10 flagged | ≤1/10 blocking-flagged | R1 59b7 (≥80% / ≤10%) |
| 2 | ≥7/10 flagged | ≤1/10 blocking-flagged | R3 3a27 |
| 3 | ≥7/10 flagged | ≤1/10 blocking-flagged | R3 3a27 |
| 4 | ≥8/10 flagged | ≤1/10 blocking-flagged | R4 03a9 |
| 5 | ≥7/10 flagged | ≤1/10 blocking-flagged | R2's operator-attested DET lint when it lands (R2 b080's OWN authoritative gate is a lexicon precision/recall eval; E2's ≥7/10 is the seeded-mutant detection target its lint is benchmarked to on this corpus) |

**AMBIGUOUS / INDETERMINATE verdict handling (the counting rule, pinned).** The production gate
emits verdict `PASS | BLOCK | INDETERMINATE` (`plan_review/__init__.py:408`; §5.1 shows INDETERMINATE
at 8% of real reviews) and per-finding `decision ∈ {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate}`. A
pure helper `classify_pair(verdict_dict, target_criteria)` applies:
- **detected (mutant, recall):** a target-family finding surfaced with `decision ∈ {block,
  advisory}` — advisory counts, because the proposed criteria ship advisory-first.
- **blocking-FP (control):** a target-family finding surfaced with `decision == block` on the
  unmutated original — advisory findings on controls are NOT false positives (advisory-first
  discipline; the Tricorder cliff is a blocking-FP bound).
- **INDETERMINATE ⇒ gate did not act:** an INDETERMINATE verdict (or a target-family finding with
  `decision == indeterminate`) counts as NOT DETECTED (FN) on a mutant and NOT a false positive on a
  control. Justification: an abstain names no defect, so it cannot be credited as a catch (this is
  the strict choice — abstains can never inflate recall) and it raises no blocking finding, so it
  cannot count against precision.
- **Run-validity guard (enforced, not advisory):** because a degenerate high-abstain run would
  spuriously read as perfect FP-avoidance, a second pure helper
  `run_validity(per_class_verdicts, indeterminate_cap=0.20)` computes, per class, the INDETERMINATE
  fraction over mutants+controls and `valid = frac <= cap` (0.20 = 2.5× the 8% historical base rate).
  The harness ALWAYS writes this to `runs/e2_run_validity.json` (`{class: {indeterminate_frac,
  valid}}`), and when ANY class is invalid it REFUSES to emit `runs/e2_baseline.json` and exits
  non-zero — so an untrustworthy run cannot silently produce a baseline table. Both pure helpers
  (`classify_pair`, `run_validity`) live in `e2_counting.py` and are golden-tested.

**Gate-execution mechanism (verified — a NEW mechanism, not a harness reuse).** The production gate
reconstructs its `PlanContext` from a STORED ticket, so E2 cannot feed it free-standing plan text;
instead it exploits that the TICKET-store root and the CODE-grounding root are SEPARABLE
(`gate_dispatch.py:141-147`; tracker precedence `config.py:119-136`; `det_floor.py` PlanContext
splits `repo_root`=CODE vs `tickets_root`=STORE). For each mutant/control the harness runs:
```
os.environ["REBAR_TRACKER_DIR"] = TMP_MUTANT_STORE   # wins verbatim over repo_root for ALL ticket reads (config.py:126-128)
os.environ["REBAR_SYNC_PUSH"] = "off"
verdict = review_plan(ticket_id, source="local", repo_root=REAL_REPO_CHECKOUT,
                      sign=False, emit_sidecar=False, force=True)
```
- **(a) ticket content = the mutant:** `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` redirects `assemble_context`'s
  `show_ticket`/`list_tickets` to the tmp mutant store.
- **(b) code-grounding = the real repo:** `source="local"` sets no code ContextVar, so
  `ctx.repo_root = resolve_code_root(repo_root=REAL_REPO_CHECKOUT)` and `cfg.repo_path` = the real
  checkout; the DET-floor absence probe (`det_floor.py:345,392`) and the agentic finders
  (`passes.py:269,514`) read the REAL modules — which is what makes class-1 (absent capability) and
  class-4 (unnecessary mechanism) grounding real. The degenerate empty-code-dir case is a SAFE
  ABSTAIN (`no_repo_root`, `det_floor.py:327-329`), never a trivial flag.
- **Non-mutating flags:** `sign=False` (no attestation), `emit_sidecar=False` (no `REVIEW_RESULT`
  write — critical so E2 does not pollute E1/R7's sidecar corpus), `force=True` (skip
  idempotent-reuse / drift-refresh so every mutant runs fresh) — `__init__.py:508-518,570-587`.
- **Mutant store = an INDEPENDENT git clone of the real store (real store untouched):** `.tickets-tracker`
  is a git **linked worktree** (created by `git worktree add`, `init.py:218,347-367`), so its `.git` is a
  FILE that points back to the real repo's `.git/worktrees/tickets` object store — a plain `cp -r` would
  leave that pointer intact, so `git commit`s run against the copy would resolve to and advance the REAL
  tickets branch (and `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` only disables `push`, not local `commit`). The harness
  therefore creates the mutant store with `git clone --local --branch tickets <REAL_REPO_CHECKOUT>
  <TMP_MUTANT_STORE>` — a standalone clone with its **own `.git` directory** and its own object store
  (every real cleanly-closed plan + its children on the `tickets` branch are present). Then, with
  `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` pointed at the clone and `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`, it injects each seeded defect into
  the clone via `rebar edit <id> --description=…` (and child edits for classes 2/3). Because the clone has
  its own object store, all mutation commits land ONLY in the clone; the real `.tickets-tracker` branch and
  origin are never written. The harness ASSERTS the clone is standalone (its `.git` is a directory, not a
  file) BEFORE any injection — so contamination is structurally prevented, not merely detected after — and
  the isolation AC additionally proves the real store's HEAD is unchanged after a run.

**Degradation if a class cannot reach 10 real base plans.** (a) One clean container seeds MULTIPLE
same-class mutants (different children re-sliced for class 2; different ordering edges deleted for
class 3), so 10 mutants ≠ 10 distinct bases — the primary path to N=10. (b) If a class still cannot
reach 10 distinct real bases, drop to the available N≥5, keep the thresholds as RATES (≥70%
detection, ≤10% blocking-FP), and record the exact shortfall N in the README — never pad with
non-clean plans. (c) If a class cannot reach N≥5 real bases (the class-2/3 container-shortage risk),
synthesize construct-valid base plans for THAT class only, label them `synthetic_base: true`, and
report those numbers separately from the real-base classes so reduced ecological validity is
visible. The measured pool (292 clean containers) makes (b)/(c) unlikely, but the ladder is pinned.

## Scope

- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/assemble_e2_mutants.py` — **mutation-assembly
  script.** Loads the outcome corpus, applies the cleanly-closed filter (+ live `closed` status +
  ≥2-children check for classes 2/3), pulls each selected ticket's plan text and children via
  `rebar` reads, applies the per-class mechanical mutation, and writes the mutant corpus with
  labels + diffs. `--dry-run` prints the selected base pool without writing.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/run_e2_benchmark.py` — **benchmark harness (distinct
  deliverable).** Creates a tmp mutant store as a standalone `git clone --local --branch tickets
  REAL_REPO → TMP_MUTANT_STORE` (own `.git` dir; NOT a `cp` of the linked worktree — see the clone
  paragraph above), injects each seeded defect into the clone via `rebar edit … --description=…` (real
  store untouched), then per
  mutant/control runs the CURRENT gate via the verified mechanism — `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`=tmp store +
  `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`, `review_plan(id, source="local", repo_root=REAL_REPO, sign=False,
  emit_sidecar=False, force=True)` (`__init__.py:387`; ticket reads hit the clone, code grounding
  reads the real checkout). Applies `classify_pair`, journals raw verdicts incrementally
  (`e2_verdicts.jsonl`, crash-resumable), computes `run_validity`, ALWAYS writes
  `e2_run_validity.json`, and emits `e2_baseline.json` ONLY when every class is valid (else exits
  non-zero). Reads its targets from `runs/e2_thresholds.json`.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e2_counting.py` — the two pure helpers `classify_pair`
  (verdict → detected/blocking-FP/indeterminate per class) and `run_validity` (INDETERMINATE-cap
  guard), with NO `anthropic`/LLM import, imported by the harness and by the unit test.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl` — committed corpus (one row per
  mutant AND per control).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_thresholds.json` — pre-registered per-class thresholds
  + the INDETERMINATE cap (committed BEFORE the baseline run).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_baseline.json` — per-class detection/FP/indeterminate
  table; `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_run_validity.json` — per-class
  `{indeterminate_frac, valid}` guard record; `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_verdicts.jsonl`
  — raw per-row gate output.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md` — E2 writeup section (method, verified gate-run
  mechanism, pre-registered thresholds, baseline results, per-R-row consumption, run-validity guard,
  store-isolation, degradation ladder).
- `tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_counting.py` — CI-collectable golden test for the two pure helpers
  in `e2_counting.py` ONLY (`classify_pair` and `run_validity`; synthetic PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE
  dicts incl. a >20%-INDETERMINATE fixture; no LLM).
- `tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_benchmark.py` — CI-collectable integration test for the
  `run_e2_benchmark.emit_baseline` enforcement seam (a distinct module from `e2_counting.py`). Drives
  `emit_baseline` with synthetic per-class verdict fixtures: a >20%-INDETERMINATE fixture asserting a
  non-zero return + no `out_path` written, and an all-valid fixture asserting return 0 + file written.
  Imports the pure `run_validity`/harness seam only; no LLM.
- **NO `src/rebar` gate/source changes** (E2 is measurement, not a gate change).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl` holds ≥10 MUTANT rows for each of the 5 defect classes (class 5
  degrades to the available N≥5 with the shortfall recorded in the README, per the class-5 ladder;
  the proving command below asserts ≥10 for classes 1–4 and the recorded N for class 5), each paired
  with exactly one CONTROL row, every row carrying `pair_id`, `defect_class`,
  `role ∈ {mutant,control}`, `source_ticket_id`, `plan_text`, `children`, and (mutants) a
  `mutation_op` + `mutation_diff`. Class-5 construct validity is checked too: every class-5 CONTROL's
  `plan_text` contains an `[operator-attested]` AC line and its paired MUTANT does not (the strip).
  Prove:
  `python3 -c "import json,collections as c,re; rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl')]; m=c.Counter(r['defect_class'] for r in rows if r['role']=='mutant'); ctl=c.Counter(r['defect_class'] for r in rows if r['role']=='control'); assert set(m)==set(ctl)=={'1','2','3','4','5'}, m; assert all(m[k]==ctl[k] for k in m) and all(m[k]>=10 for k in '1234') and m['5']>=5, (dict(m),dict(ctl)); oa=re.compile(r'\[operator-attested\]'); c5={r['pair_id']:{} for r in rows if r['defect_class']=='5'}; [c5[r['pair_id']].__setitem__(r['role'], bool(oa.search(r['plan_text']))) for r in rows if r['defect_class']=='5']; assert all(v.get('control') and not v.get('mutant') for v in c5.values()), c5; print('OK', dict(m))"`
- [ ] The cleanly-closed filter is implemented in `assemble_e2_mutants.py` exactly as
  `post_claim_edit_class=="none" AND reopen_count==0 AND force_close==False AND
  completion_verifier_fail_count==0 AND had_persisted_review==True` (+ live `closed` + ≥2 children
  for classes 2/3), and every `source_ticket_id` in the corpus satisfies it. Prove (cross-check each
  base against its corpus row):
  `python3 docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/assemble_e2_mutants.py --dry-run --verify-clean && echo FILTER-OK`
  (exits 0 only if every selected base ticket's corpus row passes the 5-condition filter; non-zero
  names the first violating ticket).
- [ ] `runs/e2_thresholds.json` pins the per-class detection target + blocking-FP bound for all 5
  classes and the INDETERMINATE run-validity cap, and is committed in a commit that PRECEDES the
  commit adding `runs/e2_baseline.json`. Prove structure + pre-registration order:
  `python3 -c "import json; t=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_thresholds.json')); assert set(t['classes'])=={'1','2','3','4','5'} and all({'detection_min','blocking_fp_max'}<=set(t['classes'][k]) for k in t['classes']) and 'indeterminate_cap' in t; print('THRESHOLDS-OK')"`
  and (strict precedence — the thresholds commit must be a DISTINCT, earlier commit than the
  baseline-run commit):
  `TH=$(git log --diff-filter=A --format=%H -1 -- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_thresholds.json); BL=$(git log --diff-filter=A --format=%H -1 -- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_baseline.json); [ "$TH" != "$BL" ] && git merge-base --is-ancestor "$TH" "$BL" && echo PRE-REGISTERED-OK`
- [ ] `run_e2_benchmark.py` runs the CURRENT gate over every mutant + control via the verified
  mechanism (`REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`=tmp mutant-store clone + `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`; `review_plan(id,
  source="local", repo_root=REAL_REPO, sign=False, emit_sidecar=False, force=True)`), writes
  `runs/e2_run_validity.json` on EVERY run, and writes `runs/e2_baseline.json` — a per-class table
  with `n_mutants, n_controls, detected, detection_rate, blocking_fp, fp_rate, indeterminate_mutants,
  indeterminate_controls, meets_threshold` — plus raw output to `runs/e2_verdicts.jsonl`, ONLY when
  every class passed the validity guard. Prove all 5 classes present with the required fields, every
  verdict in the closed vocabulary, and the committed run is valid:
  `python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_baseline.json'))['classes']; req={'n_mutants','n_controls','detected','detection_rate','blocking_fp','fp_rate','indeterminate_mutants','indeterminate_controls','meets_threshold'}; assert set(b)=={'1','2','3','4','5'} and all(req<=set(b[k]) for k in b); vs=[json.loads(l)['verdict'] for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_verdicts.jsonl')]; assert set(vs)<= {'PASS','BLOCK','INDETERMINATE'}, set(vs); v=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_run_validity.json')); assert all(v[k]['valid'] for k in v), v; print('BASELINE-OK', len(vs), 'rows')"`
- [ ] BOTH the counting rule (`e2_counting.classify_pair`: INDETERMINATE ⇒ FN on mutants, non-FP on
  controls; advisory counts for detection, only `block` counts for FP) AND the run-validity guard
  (`e2_counting.run_validity`: a class with INDETERMINATE fraction > the 0.20 cap is marked
  `valid: False`) are pure functions golden-tested with synthetic verdict dicts — including a fixture
  whose class has >20% INDETERMINATE that the test asserts `run_validity` flags invalid, and
  PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE dicts exercising every `classify_pair` branch (no LLM in the test). Prove:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_counting.py -q`
- [ ] The harness ENFORCES the guard (not merely computes it): the baseline-emit step is a testable
  seam `run_e2_benchmark.emit_baseline(per_class_verdicts, out_path) -> int` that calls
  `run_validity` and, when ANY class exceeds the 0.20 INDETERMINATE cap, **returns non-zero and does
  NOT create `out_path`** (so a degenerate high-abstain run cannot silently publish a baseline table);
  the harness `main()` propagates that non-zero as its exit code. An integration test in the distinct
  file `tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_benchmark.py` (NOT `test_e2_counting.py`, which covers only the
  `e2_counting.py` pure helpers) drives `emit_baseline` with a synthetic >20%-INDETERMINATE per-class
  fixture and a tmp `out_path`, asserting the return is non-zero AND `out_path` was not written (and,
  with an all-valid fixture, that it returns 0 and writes the file). Prove:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_benchmark.py -q`
- [ ] The benchmark run is ISOLATED from the shared store by CONSTRUCTION: the tmp mutant store is a
  standalone `git clone --local --branch tickets` with its own `.git` **directory** (not a `cp` of the
  `.tickets-tracker` linked worktree, whose `.git` is a file-pointer to the real object store), and
  `--self-check-isolation` ASSERTS `os.path.isdir(<TMP_MUTANT_STORE>/.git)` before any `rebar edit`
  injection — so a mutant commit cannot reach the real `tickets` branch even in principle. A mutant gate
  run leaves the real `.tickets-tracker` branch HEAD unchanged. Prove BOTH the standalone-clone assertion
  and that HEAD is identical before/after a single-pair run:
  `H0=$(git -C .tickets-tracker rev-parse HEAD); .venv/bin/python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/run_e2_benchmark.py --self-check-isolation; H1=$(git -C .tickets-tracker rev-parse HEAD); [ "$H0" = "$H1" ] && echo ISOLATION-OK`
- [ ] `README.md` gains an "E2 — seeded-defect benchmark" section recording the baseline results,
  the verified gate-run mechanism, the pre-registered thresholds, how R1/R3/R4/R2 each consume the
  corpus, the INDETERMINATE run-validity guard, store-isolation, and the degradation ladder. Prove
  the required sub-sections are present:
  `grep -q "E2 — seeded-defect benchmark" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md && grep -q "run-validity" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md && grep -Eq "degrad" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md && echo WRITEUP-OK`
